food_subgroup: Poultry — marinated and fried chicken pieces

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product class. Drumlets are the proximal segment of the chicken wing; no dedicated drumlet class exists. closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers cooked chicken wing broadly. No specific sweet-spicy glazed drumlet code exists; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for sweet and spicy chicken drumlets. Ontology gap noted.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 6 chicken drumlets, approximately 300 g total, as typically served at a Thai-style restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken wing, cooked, glazed / sweet-chilli style — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Chicken, wing, meat and skin, cooked, fried (NDB 05044) and Thai sweet chilli sauce component data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for sweet and spicy glazed chicken drumlets exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy NDB 05044 (chicken wing, meat and skin, fried) as the closest structural match, with an upward adjustment to carbohydrate_g and sugar_g to account for the sweet chilli glaze (approximately 8–10 g sugar per 100 g added sauce at typical coating ratio). Sodium adjusted upward to reflect fish sauce and soy-based marinade. All adaptations are conservative and documented.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed meat-and-sauce dishes estimated from analogous sweet chilli sauce and fried chicken components; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for sweet and spicy glazed chicken drumlets. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the dominant glycemic driver being sweet chilli sauce (high-sugar, moderate-GI condiment, GI ~55 for sugar-based sauces) diluted by the large protein and fat fraction of the fried chicken, which substantially blunts the glycemic response. Mixed dish GI estimation methodology follows Brand-Miller et al. (2003) published in the American Journal of Clinical Nutrition. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 28.5) / 100 = 12.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and soy-based marinade (~520 mg sodium per 100 g; ~1560 mg per 6-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium load (~1560 mg per serving, exceeding 65% of the 2400 mg daily limit), and should request reduced-sauce or sauce-on-side preparation if consumed.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~12.8 per serving) driven by sweet chilli glaze sugar content (~7.2 g sugar per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be mindful of the added sugars in the sweet chilli glaze; portion control to 3 pieces and pairing with non-starchy vegetables can help moderate the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.9 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium from marinade; deep-frying adds total fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption due to combined saturated fat and sodium burden; baked or air-fried preparation with reduced-sodium sauce is a preferable alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sweet and Spicy Chicken Drumlets (6 pieces) is a Thai-influenced dish consisting of the proximal segment of the chicken wing (drumlet), marinated in a mixture of sweet chilli sauce, fish sauce, garlic, sugar, and chilli, then deep-fried or baked until the skin is crispy and the glaze is caramelised. The dish is characterised by a sticky, glossy coating that delivers a balance of sweetness and moderate heat, with a juicy, tender interior and crispy exterior. It is commonly served as a starter, sharing plate, or side dish in Thai restaurants and casual dining establishments in Singapore. Nutritionally, each 6-piece serving (approximately 300 g) provides around 729 kcal, 61.5 g protein, 42.6 g fat, and 28.5 g carbohydrate, with notable sodium content (~1560 mg) from the fish sauce and soy-based marinade. The dish is high in protein and moderate in fat, making it satiating, but the sodium and added sugar content from the glaze are clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The sweet chilli glaze contributes a moderate glycemic load of approximately 12.8 per serving. This dish is halal-suitable and dairy-free but contains fish allergens from fish sauce.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for sweet and spicy glazed chicken drumlets exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy fried chicken wing data with documented adjustments for the sweet chilli glaze. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is medium rather than low because the base food (fried chicken wing) is well-characterised in USDA SR Legacy and the glaze adjustment is conservative and methodologically documented.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for fried chicken wing and is not reliably estimated for this preparation. All other -1 fields are not applicable or not reported in the adapted source. Nutrient values represent a per-100g estimate for the combined dish (chicken plus glaze) at a typical restaurant serving ratio. The serving size of 300 g (6 drumlets) is an estimate based on average drumlet weight of approximately 50 g per piece; actual restaurant portions may vary. The dish is listed under Thai cuisine as served at Shrimp Prawn Seafood - Boat Quay on field survey data, though the preparation style is consistent with Thai sweet chilli chicken dishes broadly. Halal status is assumed based on typical restaurant practice but not independently verified. Gluten-free status is marked false due to possible soy sauce in marinade.

Added Chinese simplified and traditional names as supplementary local names.
Populated all per_100g nutrient fields using USDA SR Legacy NDB 05044 as base with documented glaze adaptation.
Documented vitamin_d_mcg = -1 with explicit justification in notes per G3 sentinel value rule.
Estimated GI at 45 (low) using analogous estimate methodology with peer-reviewed citation.
Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.

Set confidence to medium with documented rationale.
